timeline.title: Univeristy of Auckland Student
timeline.description: Tertiary education, studying Computer Systems Engineering.

details.body: Throughout my Computer Systems Engineering degree at the University of Auckland, I worked on a range of projects spanning embedded systems, native apps, and full-stack web development. Browse the slideshow below to see a few highlights.

links.label: A Mechatronics and a Computer Systems Student Walk into a Software Development Internship...
The Subtle Art of Programming Like a Pro-grammer

timeline.title: Pushpay Intern
timeline.description: Node.js Software Development Engineer Internship via Summer of Tech

timeline.title: Engineering Revue
timeline.description: Dancer to Choreographer to Dance Director and TRON tech lead.

details.body: I had auditioned for the University of Auckland's Engineering Revue in my first year and was lucky enough to join the cast as a dancer. I progressed to choreographer in my third year, choreographing a big jazz group and became the technical lead for TRON through the midst of covid. In my final year, I became Dance Director and continued leading the tech behind TRON whilst mentoring our up and coming tech lead. I choreographed the boy's dance and girl's and they's dance alongside my fellow choreographers. TRON was a signature dance act combining synchronised music and lighting with custom electrical and software components. Each year the technology was rebuilt and refined to match the show's theme.

links.label: You Know They're Out There 2020 (TRON Tech)

timeline.title: BEng (Hons) in Computer Systems Engineering
timeline.description: Officially graduated with First Class Honours in Computer Systems Engineering.

timeline.title: Generosity Award Winner

insights.heading: Insights
insights.passwordPrompt: 🔒 Enter the password to view this section.

tron.title: TRON
tron.subTitle: Technical Lead
tron.text: Combining dance, synchronised music and lighting, TRON is a dance act performed in the University of Auckland's Engineering Revue stage show production. The technology is comprised of electrical and software components which were maintained and updated yearly in accordance to the show's theme.

yumble.title: Yumble
yumble.subTitle: Front-end Web Developer
yumble.text: Yumble is an application designed to help you find good with your friends. An innovative combination of dating apps such as Tinder and Bumble, with fast paced competitive apps such as Kahoot, Yumble helps you and your friends find food, fast. Project developed for SOFTENG 701 to work in teams of ~20 students aiming to develop skills in advanced software engineering development methods.

kafeKrayon.title: Kafe Krayon
kafeKrayon.subTitle: Front-end Web Developer
kafeKrayon.text: Kafe Krayon is a web application that allows users to provide a location (e.g. Panmure, Ponsonby) that shows them the cafes around the area, as well as provide Google Map Markers that show them where the cafes are. This project was designed and developed over the 2020 Create Camp hackathon weekend hosted by Summer of Tech and ANZ. This application uses the ZenbuAPI for location data, as well as a GoogleMaps API to show users the location of the cafes.

recordLine.title: Record Line
recordLine.subTitle: Native Developer
recordLine.text: Record Line is an Android application centred as an E-commerce marketplace that revolves around the sales of vinyl albums for potential users who love to listen to the classics the old-fashioned way. Project developed for COMPSYS 302 to develop a deeper understanding of system modelling in order to produce a user-friendly and aesthetic application. More work is to be done to allow the application to work with an appropriate dataset.

freqRelay.title: Frequency Relay
freqRelay.subTitle: Embedded Systems Design Engineer
freqRelay.text: A low-cost frequency relay application which controls load tripping according to the measured frequency of the power network. The functionality of this frequency relay network was split based on the logical concurrent operations of the network. Project developed for COMPSYS 723 aimed to implement the software application of a frequency relay, targeted for use in normal households. The software controls load tripping according to the measured frequency of the power network.
